title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow
input_row.description: Do not always authorize all requests.
input_row.rationale: The API Server, can be configured to allow all requests. This mode should not be used on any production cluster. Impact: Only authorized requests will be served.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --authorization-mode argument exists and is not set to AlwaysAllow.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --authorization-mode parameter to values other than AlwaysAllow. One such example could be as below. --authorization-mode=RBAC Default Value: By default, AlwaysAllow is not enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authorization/
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node
input_row.impact: Only authorized requests will be served.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_authorization_mode_always_allow_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to verify that the --authorization-mode flag does not include AlwaysAllow.
- If the cluster uses static pods (common in kubeadm and many managed clusters), retrieve the kube-apiserver manifest:
  - Use: kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml to identify the pod, or directly inspect the /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml file if accessible.
  - Alternatively, use: kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}' to extract the command line arguments.
- Parse the command line arguments for --authorization-mode.
- Ensure that:
  - The --authorization-mode flag is present.
  - The value does NOT include AlwaysAllow (case-sensitive).
  - Acceptable values include RBAC, Node, Webhook, ABAC, etc., but not AlwaysAllow.
- Pass Criteria: --authorization-mode is set and AlwaysAllow is NOT present in the list of modes.
- Fail Criteria: --authorization-mode is missing or includes AlwaysAllow.
- Example check logic:
  - For each kube-apiserver pod/container, extract the command line.
  - Search for --authorization-mode.
  - Split the value by commas and confirm AlwaysAllow is not present.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
